about.title: About Me
about.bio: Java developer in training at IT-Högskolan, focused on backend development, system design, and software architecture. Have delivered a production system for a non-profit organization and have basic experience with frontend development in React. Seeking a junior role in backend or system development.
experience: Web Developer — Fishing Club (volunteer, production system)
Youth Leader & Board Member — Fishing Club (ongoing)

items.title: BookingAPI
items.problem: No existing booking backend fit the needs of small organizations managing room or event reservations with authentication and conflict detection.
items.solution: REST API for booking management with JWT authentication, role-based access control, and database-driven conflict detection. Fully containerized and tested with unit and integration tests.
highlights: Business logic for booking conflicts with database-level exclusion constraint
JWT-based stateless authentication with role-based access control
Database schema with 4 tables, foreign key constraints, and Flyway migrations
Unit and integration tests with Testcontainers against real PostgreSQL

architectureDecisions.design: Layered architecture (Controller → Service → Repository) with clear responsibility per layer. Global @RestControllerAdvice provides consistent error responses.
architectureDecisions.database: PostgreSQL with four tables. Exclusion constraint prevents overlapping bookings at the database level. Flyway manages schema migrations.
architectureDecisions.scalability: Stateless JWT approach with no server-side sessions. HikariCP connection pooling for concurrent database access.
architectureDecisions.tradeoffs: JWT over session-based auth for statelessness — accepted that token revocation requires additional infrastructure. PostgreSQL over H2 for production fidelity, with Docker Compose for local development.

items.title: Fishing Club Website
items.problem: The fishing club had no digital presence — members relied on word-of-mouth and new members couldn't find the club online.
items.solution: Built and deployed the club's official website. Managed the full lifecycle from requirements gathering to deployment. Production system with real users.
highlights: Delivered a production system for a real organization
Managed the full project lifecycle: requirements, design, development, deployment

architectureDecisions.design: Static site architecture for maximum reliability and zero maintenance overhead.
architectureDecisions.database: No database dependency — content managed through static files to minimize operational complexity.
architectureDecisions.scalability: Static hosting via CDN handles any realistic traffic spike.
architectureDecisions.tradeoffs: Prioritized simplicity over dynamic features. A CMS would have enabled non-technical editing but added unjustified complexity.

items.title: Järnvilja
items.problem: School assignment to build a fullstack application demonstrating end-to-end development from database design to user interface.
items.solution: Fullstack web application with a martial arts theme. Backend API with layered architecture, relational database, and server-rendered frontend via Thymeleaf.
highlights: RESTful API with clear endpoint structure and CRUD operations
Normalized relational database design with foreign key constraints
Clear separation of concerns between presentation and business logic

architectureDecisions.design: Layered architecture separating HTTP handling, business logic, and data access. Backend exposes a REST API that can serve any client.
architectureDecisions.database: Normalized tables with foreign key constraints for referential integrity at the database level.
architectureDecisions.scalability: Stateless API design enables horizontal scaling.
architectureDecisions.tradeoffs: Monolithic deployment for simplicity given the project scope.

items.title: Weather App
items.problem: Needed to demonstrate the ability to consume external REST APIs and handle asynchronous data flows.
items.solution: Weather application integrating with OpenWeatherMap API. Focus on clean API integration patterns and structured error handling.
highlights: REST API consumption with structured error handling and loading states
Data transformation from complex API responses to clean UI components

architectureDecisions.design: API communication separated into a dedicated service module, decoupled from UI components.
architectureDecisions.database: No database — intentionally stateless, fetching fresh data on each request.
architectureDecisions.scalability: Client-side application. A production version would route calls through a backend proxy.
architectureDecisions.tradeoffs: API calls directly from the browser for simplicity — exposes API key client-side. Better pattern applied in BookingAPI.

items.title: AI Chatbot
items.problem: Wanted to explore integrating large language models into applications and how prompt engineering affects response quality.
items.solution: Chatbot integrating with OpenAI's API. Handles conversation state, prompt templates, and structured error handling.
highlights: LLM API integration via HTTP client with error handling
Conversation state management preserving context across exchanges
API key security via environment variables

architectureDecisions.design: Clear separation between conversation handler, API client, and prompt templates. Easy to swap LLM provider.
architectureDecisions.scalability: Single-user design. Production version would require message queue and rate limiting.
architectureDecisions.tradeoffs: Synchronous API calls for simplicity over streaming responses.
